profiles.name: Zubayr Charles
profiles.shortBio: Sets the publication vision, commissions new work, and guides each Babas & Brasse edition.

profiles.name: Zoe Petersen
profiles.shortBio: Builds the reading experience, editorial tools, accessibility systems, and production release.

profiles.name: Naledi Maseko
profiles.shortBio: Shapes the editorial calendar and works with writers from first pitch through final publication.

profiles.name: Ayesha Daniels
profiles.shortBio: Commissions photography and artwork that gives each story a distinct visual language.

profiles.name: Lerato Mokoena
profiles.shortBio: Writes intimate cultural essays about hospitality, daily rituals, and the changing shape of community.

profiles.name: Sihle Ndlovu
profiles.shortBio: Covers rehearsal processes, performance, and the designers building South African stages.

profiles.name: Mia van Wyk
profiles.shortBio: Reviews fiction and criticism with particular attention to translation and multilingual craft.

profiles.name: Thando Jacobs
profiles.shortBio: Writes about belonging, language, and the creative communities making culture across the Cape.

articles.title: Send A Text Before You Knock
articles.dek: On privacy, hospitality, and why arriving at someone's door now begins with a message.

featuredImage.title: On Belonging
featuredImage.altText: Neighbors talking in a colorful Cape Town residential street
featuredImage.caption: Community, memory, and everyday ideas of home.

bodyBlocks: A knock at the door used to be ordinary. Now it can feel like a small interruption in a life coordinated by messages.
This essay considers privacy, hospitality, and the changing rituals around arriving in someone else's space.

seo.title: Send A Text Before You Knock | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: An essay on privacy, hospitality, and the changing rituals of arrival.
seo.ogTitle: Send A Text Before You Knock
seo.ogDescription: Privacy, hospitality, and the changing rituals of arrival.

articles.title: Review: New Voices at the Baxter
articles.dek: A draft review of an emerging theatre programme balancing formal ambition with raw new voices.

featuredImage.title: The Rehearsal Room
featuredImage.altText: A South African theatre ensemble rehearsing on a black-box stage
featuredImage.caption: A new generation finding its voice in rehearsal.

bodyBlocks: The programme puts emerging directors beside established makers and lets their differences remain visible.
This draft considers where the evening finds a shared theatrical language and where it productively resists one.

seo.title: Review: New Voices at the Baxter | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: A draft review of the New Voices theatre programme at the Baxter.
seo.ogTitle: Review: New Voices at the Baxter
seo.ogDescription: Emerging theatre-makers test a shared stage language.

articles.title: Inside the Rehearsal Room
articles.dek: Five theatre-makers talk about trust, process, and the difficult work of building an ensemble.

featuredImage.title: The Language Workshop
featuredImage.altText: A diverse writing group discussing pages around a table
featuredImage.caption: Multilingual cultural work built through conversation.

bodyBlocks: A rehearsal room is built from attention before it is built from scenery.
These theatre-makers describe the rituals, disagreements, and shared language behind an ensemble.

seo.title: Inside the Rehearsal Room | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: A conversation with South African theatre-makers.
seo.ogDescription: A conversation from the rehearsal room.

articles.title: The Revival of Afrikaans Theatre
articles.dek: A new generation of playwrights is reimagining traditional narratives for contemporary South African stages.

featuredImage.title: The Rehearsal Room
featuredImage.altText: A South African theatre ensemble rehearsing on a black-box stage
featuredImage.caption: A new generation finding its voice in rehearsal.

bodyBlocks: Young theatre-makers are bringing fresh perspectives to familiar forms.
Their work joins tradition and experimentation without treating either as decoration.

seo.title: The Revival of Afrikaans Theatre | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: A look at a new generation on South African stages.
seo.ogDescription: A new generation takes the stage.

articles.title: Between Languages: The Craft of a South African Novel
articles.dek: On literary craftsmanship, translation, and the playful possibilities of language.

featuredImage.title: Between Languages
featuredImage.altText: A reader turning a page at a book-lined wooden table
featuredImage.caption: Reading, translation, and the material life of books.

bodyBlocks: Language shapes both the rhythm and the reach of a novel.
Reading between languages can reveal what a single vocabulary leaves unsaid.

seo.title: Between Languages | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: Literary craft across South African languages.

articles.title: On Belonging: Reflections from the Cape Flats
articles.dek: A personal essay about identity, community, memory, and the meaning of home.

featuredImage.title: On Belonging
featuredImage.altText: Neighbors talking in a colorful Cape Town residential street
featuredImage.caption: Community, memory, and everyday ideas of home.

bodyBlocks: Belonging is personal, communal, and political.
The stories of home carry contradiction as honestly as they carry affection.

articles.title: Why Multilingualism Matters
articles.dek: An argument for cultural spaces that reflect the languages South Africans actually live in.

featuredImage.title: The Language Workshop
featuredImage.altText: A diverse writing group discussing pages around a table
featuredImage.caption: Multilingual cultural work built through conversation.

bodyBlocks: Multilingualism is a creative resource, not a problem to solve.
Our cultural institutions should make room for the full texture of public language.

seo.title: Why Multilingualism Matters | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: A case for multilingual cultural spaces.

articles.title: Stagecraft and Storytelling
articles.dek: How set design shapes the emotional and physical world of a performance.

featuredImage.title: Stagecraft and Storytelling
featuredImage.altText: A theatre designer adjusting a miniature stage model in a workshop
featuredImage.caption: The craft and material imagination behind a performance.

bodyBlocks: A set is never only a backdrop.
The best stagecraft reveals meaning through space, material, light, and movement.

seo.title: Stagecraft and Storytelling | Babas & Brasse
seo.description: The art of local set design.

reviews.name: Reviewer
reviews.body: A warm, precise essay that turns an ordinary social habit into a larger reflection on care and boundaries.

reviews.name: Reviewer
reviews.body: This response was held for moderation because it did not address the published work.
